kept.text:
Revolut has formally joined the Associação Portuguesa de Bancos (APB) — the main Portuguese banking association.

This move marks a significant step in Revolut’s transition from fintech to a full-fledged banking player in Portugal. It strengthens the company’s long-term commitment to the local market and improves collaboration with traditional banks on innovation, financial inclusion, and digitalisation.

kept.text:
Revolut is gradually launching Mercados Revolut - its Stock trading platform in Mexico.

The service, initially available to Metal and Premium account holders, allows customers to invest in over 2,000 US stocks and ETFs from as little as $1.

Revolut says this execution-only service is routed through Revolut Singapore and partners for execution on U.S. markets.

The service is commission-free till June.

#Mexico

kept.text:
Revolut is still eyeing Morocco — just not with any noticeable enthusiasm.

Revolut has not walked away from Morocco, but the latest moves suggest distinctly low expectations. Back in September 2025, when the company mapped out plans to enter more than 30 new markets by 2030 and singled out South Africa as its main African bet, Morocco was quietly omitted despite earlier exploratory moves.

A payment services licence application has reportedly been filed with Bank Al-Maghrib, yet zero public updates on its status have emerged. Team expansion on the ground has been equally unhurried — the kind of measured pace that signals this is not a priority market right now.

Yesterday, Revolut Morocco CEO Yacine Faqir flagged a new role: Head of Legal – Morocco. The brief demands local-law qualifications (or substantial experience advising under Moroccan law) and direct familiarity with how the regulatory regime applies to digital banks. Core responsibilities include "advising on compliance and legal issues for structuring new and existing financial products" and "monitoring and handling regulatory developments and liaising with external counsel on expansion into new market verticals."

This signals to me that they have not given up on Morocco, but sparse moves suggest a lack of momentum and modest expectations by the company.

Over 65 candidates have already applied for this role on LinkedIn. It seems there is no shortage of people wanting to work for Revolut, but the hurdle for entering Morocco is the local licence.

What is your take on Revolut in Morocco?

#Morocco

Good Evening X fam✨

Revolut gave you an app.

Monzo gave you a card.

Chime gave you a pastel UI.

None of them gave you your keys.

The interface changed. The cage didn’t.

@useTria rebuilds the rails ,self-custodial, no bridges, no custodians, 150+ countries, 1,000+ tokens.

Not a better gate,A better foundation.

profile_bio.description: Passionate about Crypto |Building vibes & engagement

kept.text:
This thread just described the most important shift in finance nobody is talking about loudly enough.

Let me add what's sitting underneath it.

- Revolut didn't disrupt banking. It redecorated it.

- Same frozen accounts. Same "we're reviewing your transaction" emails at 2am. Same three business days that somehow stretch into five. 

- They removed the queue and gave you a nicer notification when things went wrong.

- That was never the revolution. That was the preview.

- The actual revolution is when you stop asking the bank's permission to use your own money.

- That's where @useTria lives.

- Your balance. Your keys. Your yield earned natively, not gifted by some institution deciding what percentage of your own money you deserve back.

-  A Visa card spending across 150+ countries without a custodian quietly sitting in the middle, taking a cut, and calling it a service.

- 500K+ people are already here. Not experimenting. Not testing. Living it daily spending, real transactions, actual money moving the way it always should have.

- Wave 1 sold you a better-looking cage.

- Wave 2 doesn't have a cage.

- That's not a small distinction. That's the entire game and the people who understand it earliest are already on the winning side of it.

- Powered by @MindoAI

profile_bio.description: Web3 Guy | Copy Writer | Ghost Writer | Community Builder | Creator @SeedifyFund | Team @Syndicate_xyz | Inner Circle @ActionModelAI | Alpha Content & Thread 🧵

quoted_tweet.text:
The first wave of neobanks unbundled the branch.

Revolut, Monzo, Chime, N26 - billions raised to ship a banking app instead of a teller. 

But the custodian, the SWIFT rails, the 3-day cross-border settlement, the "your account is under review" emails - all still there underneath.

profile_bio.description: Self-custodial neobank unifying spend, trade, and earn. Lowest fees across 150+ countries. Supercharged by BestPath.
